items.title: How do I deposit DOLA to mint phUSD?
items.body: Enter the amount of DOLA you want to deposit. If this is your first time, you'll need to approve the smart contract to spend your DOLA tokens. After approval, click Deposit to mint phUSD at the current bonding curve price.

items.title: What is the current exchange rate?
items.body: The exchange rate is determined by the bonding curve and changes as more tokens are minted. You'll see the exact rate displayed below the amount input, along with the minimum amount you'll receive based on your slippage tolerance.

items.title: Why do I need to approve tokens first?
items.body: Token approval is a standard security measure in DeFi. It grants the smart contract permission to spend your DOLA tokens. You only need to approve once, and the approval remains valid for future deposits.

items.title: What is slippage tolerance?
items.body: Slippage tolerance is the maximum price change you're willing to accept. If the bonding curve price moves beyond this tolerance before your transaction is confirmed, the transaction will revert to protect you from unfavorable rates.

items.title: What is phUSD?
items.body: phUSD is Phoenix's Evergreen Stablecoin — a new kind of stablecoin whose backing grows stronger over time. Phoenix takes yield from the wider DeFi ecosystem, captures it permanently using protocol-owned liquidity, and uses that yield in the Behodler ecosystem. For more info, see: phUSD Overview (https://github.com/Behodler/docs-public/blob/main/phUSD.md)

items.title: How do I mint phUSD?
items.body: Connect your wallet, deposit DOLA, and the bonding curve mints phUSD at the current curve price. During Phase 1 (Ignition), the curve price gradually increases toward $1. When parity is reached, the bonding curve disables and phUSD mints at a fixed $1 price. For more info on Phase 1, see: Phoenix Phase 1 — Ignition (https://github.com/Behodler/docs-public/blob/main/Phoenix%20Phase%201%3A%20Ignition%20%E2%80%94%20Capitalising%20phUSD%20with%20the%20Behodler%203%20Bonding%20Curve.md)

items.title: Why can't I use ETH/USDC etc to mint phUSD?
items.body: Phoenix requires DOLA because all minting must flow into the AutoDOLA Vault. While Phoenix could auto-swap other tokens (ETH, USDC, etc.) into DOLA, adding that routing at launch would have required additional development time and delayed release. Future versions may support multi-asset minting, but for now phUSD mints exclusively from DOLA.

items.title: What backs phUSD?
items.body: All phUSD minted through the bonding curve is backed by DOLA deposited into the AutoDOLA Vault. This vault compounds yield and provides the foundation for phUSD's stability.

items.title: What is the AutoDOLA Vault?
items.body: The AutoDOLA vault is a yield vault provided by the Autofinance team (formerly Tokemak). It accepts DOLA and automatically stakes it into sDOLA to generate sustainable on-chain yield. Phoenix uses this vault as the destination for all DOLA minted through phUSD, turning that yield into permanent backing and liquidity. For more info on Auto Pools, see: Autofinance Auto Pools TL;DR (https://docs.auto.finance/auto-pools-protocol/autopools-tl-dr)

items.title: Where can I use phUSD?
items.body: During Phase 1, phUSD can be traded on the bonding curve. In later stages, phUSD can be staked to earn yield, and as supply grows, additional integrations across DeFi will become available.

items.title: How do withdrawals work?
items.body: Enter the amount of phUSD you want to burn to withdraw DOLA. The system calculates how much DOLA you'll receive based on the current bonding curve price, minus the withdrawal fee. Click Withdraw to complete the transaction.

items.title: How long does a withdrawal take?
items.body: Withdrawals are processed immediately on the blockchain. Once your transaction is confirmed (typically 12-15 seconds), you'll receive your DOLA tokens. The actual time may vary based on network congestion.

items.title: Can I withdraw any amount?
items.body: Yes, you can withdraw any amount up to your total phUSD balance. Use the Max button to automatically select your entire balance for withdrawal if you want to exit your position completely.

items.title: Is the slippage on Withdraw worse than on deposit
items.body: The deposits are stored in vaults owned and controlled by external protocols. Depending on the vault, there may be additional slippage but this is usually less than 0.1%.

items.title: Can I redeem phUSD or exit the system?
items.body: Yes. You can sell your phUSD on the bonding curve to redeem DOLA.

items.title: Can the system be paused? If so, what happens to my funds?
items.body: Yes. Anyone can burn 1,000 EYE to trigger a temporary pause if they detect a severe issue. During a Pause, minting and redeeming are disabled, but funds inside the vault remain safe. The Pause is removed once the issue is resolved.

items.title: What is the Ignition Phase?
items.body: The Ignition Phase is the initial launch period of the bonding curve where phUSD can be minted at prices below the target $1.00 peg. Early participants benefit from lower prices that gradually increase as more tokens are minted.

items.title: How does the bonding curve work?
items.body: The bonding curve is a mathematical formula that determines the price of phUSD based on the total supply minted. The price starts at a lower value and increases along the curve until it reaches the final target price of $1.00.

items.title: What does the progress percentage mean?
items.body: The progress percentage shows how far along the bonding curve we've traveled from the start price to the end price. Higher progress means a higher current price and less remaining opportuni0y for early-adopter pricing benefits.

items.title: Why does the current price keep changing?
items.body: The current price changes with each deposit as more phUSD tokens are minted. The bonding curve algorithm automatically adjusts the price upward, creating a fair price discovery mechanism that rewards early participation.

items.title: Does the dapp lock when the price reaches $1
items.body: Not automatically. The protocol has to be manually locked.

items.title: What if the price never reaches $1
items.body: The $1 price is a limit, not a goal. Ending the ignition phase on a lower final price simply confers more early adopter benefit to those who bought.
